content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN **positive** integers.

In one operation, you can do the following:


Choose an index iii (1≤i≤N)(1 \le i \le N)(1≤i≤N), and change the value of AiA_iAi​ to either (Ai)2(A_i)^2(Ai​)2 or ⌊Ai⌋\lfloor \sqrt{A_i} \rfloor⌊Ai​​⌋.
Find the **minimum** number of operations required, such that, for the final array AAA:


AAA is sorted in **non-decreasing** order;
AN≤1018A_N \le 10^{18}AN​≤1018.
**NOTE:** The elements of the array might not fit in a 32-bit integer.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input:

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN integers, A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​ - the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum number of operations required.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the size of the array. The next line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of operations required to make the array non‑decreasing.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First case already sorted. Second case: sqrt(9)=3 makes array non‑decreasing. Third case needs three sqrt operations as described in the statement.
